task_completion.description: Verify the FZK-Haus architectural model is loaded in the BIM software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender/Bonsai BIM interface is open, displaying a 3D architectural house model (FZK-Haus) in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify a new IFC Material named 'Audit_Blue' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Material properties panel, outliner, or IFC material manager shows a material explicitly named 'Audit_Blue'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Audit_Blue' material has an IFC Surface Style with a predominantly blue diffuse color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Color picker or material surface style settings show a blue color selected for the diffuse channel, where the blue value is visibly higher than the red and green values.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Audit_Blue' material is assigned to at least 4 IfcWall entities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows at least 4 distinct walls rendered in blue, or the UI shows the 'Audit_Blue' material actively assigned to multiple IfcWall objects in the properties/outliner.

task_completion.description: Verify the updated IFC project is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog, export menu, or terminal confirmation showing the file being saved exactly to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_color_coded.ifc'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Blender/Bonsai BIM graphical interface to perform the material authoring and assignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with Blender's 3D viewport, material properties tabs, and outliner, rather than exclusively using a terminal script.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual FZK-Haus model.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D model visible in the software is a complete house with walls, doors, and windows, not a simple default cube, empty scene, or unrelated geometry.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually edit the IFC file in a text editor to fake the material assignments.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) is shown being used to manually write raw IFC STEP physical file lines to bypass the BIM software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_color_coded.ifc'. The required material name is exactly 'Audit_Blue'. The material must be assigned to at least 4 'IfcWall' entities. The diffuse color must have a Blue RGB channel significantly higher than Red and Green (specifically, Blue > Red * 1.2 and Blue > Green * 1.2).
